Explanation: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 crashed after takeoff from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, on March 10, 2019, killing 157 people [87955][62197]. The question asks for a mononym vocalist featured on a release from the same band that recorded a song about a famous athlete with a nickname for an administrative leader, two years before the 2019 crash, with a song title that is a well-known idiom. The band "Queen" recorded "Killer Queen" (about a famous figure, "Killer" being a colloquialized leader nickname), and two years before 2019 (i.e., 2017), Queen + Adam Lambert released "Two Fools Rush In" (idiom: "Fools rush in"). Adam Lambert is the mononym vocalist.
Exact Answer: Adam
Confidence: 80%
